name: No Strategic Portfolio Management — High Operational Volume
description: Instance has no SPM/ITBM but has significant change and incident volume, indicating operational complexity that would benefit from portfolio governance.
condition_desc: SPM absent AND (500+ change requests OR 2000+ incidents) — operational complexity without portfolio oversight
message_template: No SPM but {change_request_count} changes + {incident_count} incidents. Operational complexity without portfolio governance.
recommendation: Enable Strategic Portfolio Management (SPM) to cover the Strategy to Portfolio (S2P) value stream. With {change_request_count} changes and {incident_count} incidents, this instance has significant operational complexity that needs demand management, portfolio prioritization, and investment planning to align IT delivery with business strategy.

name: No GRC — Multi-Product Instance Without Governance
description: Instance runs multiple ServiceNow products but has no GRC capability. Multi-product footprints require governance and risk oversight.
condition_desc: GRC absent AND 2+ products active (ITSM, CSM, HRSD, SecOps) — multi-product footprint without governance
message_template: No GRC but multiple products active (ITSM + CSM/HRSD/SecOps). Multi-product footprint without governance oversight.
recommendation: Enable GRC (Integrated Risk Management) to provide governance, risk assessment, and compliance monitoring. With multiple active products, this instance needs centralized risk and compliance oversight to ensure regulatory alignment across all workflows.

name: No Change Management — Active Operations Without Deployment Control
description: Instance has significant incident volume but no Change Management, indicating active operations without controlled deployment processes.
condition_desc: Change Management absent AND 500+ incidents — active operations without deployment control
message_template: No Change Management but {incident_count} incidents. Active operations without deployment control.
recommendation: Enable Change Management to provide controlled release and deployment processes. With {incident_count} incidents, this instance has active operations that need deployment governance as part of the R2D value stream.

name: No CMDB for Configuration Tracking
description: CMDB is not populated. R2D requires configuration item tracking for deployments.
condition_desc: cmdb_ci table has fewer than 100 records or Discovery is not active
message_template: CMDB has only {total_cis} CIs and no Discovery. R2D lacks configuration tracking.
recommendation: Populate the CMDB via Discovery or Service Mapping to track configuration items that are affected by deployments. A well-maintained CMDB is critical for R2D.

name: No Service Catalog — ITSM Active Without Structured Request Intake
description: Instance has active ITSM operations but no Service Catalog, meaning requests arrive through unstructured channels (email, walk-ups, direct tickets).
condition_desc: Service Catalog absent AND ITSM active with 200+ incidents — request volume without structured intake
message_template: ITSM active with {incident_count} incidents but no Service Catalog. Requests lack structured intake.
recommendation: Enable Service Catalog to provide structured request intake. With {incident_count} incidents, this instance has significant ticket volume that would benefit from categorized request forms, approval workflows, and fulfillment automation.

name: No Self-Service Portal — Service Catalog Without User Channel
description: Instance has Service Catalog but no portal for end-users to access it. Catalog items exist but users have no self-service front door.
condition_desc: Service Catalog active but neither Service Portal nor Employee Center installed — catalog items exist without a user-facing channel
message_template: Service Catalog active but no self-service portal. Users cannot browse or submit catalog requests.
recommendation: Deploy Service Portal or Employee Center to provide end-users with a self-service channel. Your Service Catalog has items but no front door for users to browse and submit requests.

name: No Knowledge Base for Self-Resolution
description: Knowledge Base is not active. R2F benefits from self-resolution to reduce ticket volume.
condition_desc: Knowledge plugin (com.glideapp.knowledge) is not installed or kb_knowledge table has fewer than 10 published articles
message_template: Knowledge Base has {kb_knowledge_count} articles. Self-resolution limited.
recommendation: Enable and populate the Knowledge Base to allow end-users to self-resolve common issues, reducing ticket volume in the R2F stream.

name: No Problem Management — High Incident Volume
description: Instance has significant incident volume but no Problem Management. Without root cause analysis, the same incidents recur.
condition_desc: Problem Management absent AND 1000+ incidents — high ticket volume without root cause analysis loop
message_template: No Problem Management but {incident_count} incidents. High ticket volume without root cause analysis.
recommendation: Enable Problem Management to perform root cause analysis and prevent recurring incidents. With {incident_count} incidents and no problem management, this instance lacks the reactive-to-proactive loop that the D2C value stream requires.

name: No Event Management — Reactive Operations at Scale
description: Instance has high incident volume but no Event Management or monitoring integration, indicating purely reactive operations at significant scale.
condition_desc: Event Management absent AND 5000+ incidents — reactive operations at scale without proactive monitoring
message_template: No Event Management but {incident_count} incidents. Reactive operations at scale — consider event-driven monitoring.
recommendation: Enable ITOM Event Management or integrate with external monitoring tools. With {incident_count} incidents and no event correlation, this instance discovers problems only when users report them. Event-driven detection (per Jochen Geist's decision tree) provides real-time alerting and correlation to reduce MTTR and prevent incidents.

name: Single Value Stream Coverage
description: Instance only covers one IT4IT value stream. Mature organizations cover all four.
condition_desc: Active plugins only map to a single IT4IT value stream (S2P, R2D, R2F, or D2C)
message_template: Only {covered_count} IT4IT value stream(s) covered: {covered_streams}.
recommendation: Consider expanding ServiceNow usage across additional IT4IT value streams to maximize platform ROI and operational maturity.

name: Heavy JDBC Usage — REST API Preferred
description: Instance has multiple JDBC data sources. Per Jochen Geist's decision tree, Web Services (REST) are the preferred default for 80% of integrations. JDBC is a fallback pattern for legacy systems or bulk operations only.
condition_desc: 3+ JDBC data sources found — significant reliance on JDBC fallback pattern
message_template: {sys_data_source_count} JDBC data sources detected. Heavy reliance on JDBC fallback pattern — review for REST API migration.
recommendation: Prefer REST API (Scripted REST or Standard REST) over JDBC for real-time integrations. JDBC should only be used for bulk data operations where REST is not feasible. Per the Integration Pattern Decision Tree: REST is the default Web Service pattern; JDBC is a fallback solution.

name: SOAP When REST Available
description: Instance uses SOAP web services where REST endpoints may be available.
condition_desc: Active SOAP messages found — modern integrations should prefer REST
message_template: {sys_soap_message_count} SOAP message configurations detected. Consider migrating to REST.
recommendation: Migrate SOAP integrations to REST where the target system supports it. REST is lighter weight, easier to maintain, and the preferred pattern for modern ServiceNow integrations.

name: Custom Integrations Without Integration Hub
description: Instance has significant custom REST message configurations but no Integration Hub. Per Jochen Geist: "OOTB first, always — check native integrations, spokes, and frameworks before custom development."
condition_desc: 5+ custom REST messages configured but Integration Hub is not installed — OOTB-first principle violated
message_template: {sys_rest_message_count} custom REST messages but no Integration Hub. OOTB-first principle violated — check available spokes.
recommendation: Use Integration Hub spokes instead of custom scripted integrations. Spokes provide built-in error handling, retry logic, connection management, and are maintained by ServiceNow or spoke vendors. Per the Integration Design methodology: "Don't build what already exists."

name: Bidirectional Sync Without Conflict Resolution
description: Two-way data sync detected — both inbound (import sets) and outbound (REST/SOAP messages) integrations exist, suggesting bidirectional sync.
condition_desc: Import sets and outbound REST/SOAP both exist, suggesting bidirectional sync without a clear data master
message_template: Bidirectional integration detected: {sys_import_set_count} import sets + outbound REST/SOAP messages. Verify conflict resolution strategy.
recommendation: Define a clear system of record for each data entity. Use coalesce fields on import sets and implement conflict resolution rules. Bidirectional sync without a defined master leads to data inconsistencies.

name: Inbound Data Without Transform Maps
description: Import sets exist without corresponding transform maps for data cleansing.
condition_desc: sys_import_set records exist but sys_transform_map is empty
message_template: {sys_import_set_count} import sets found but no transform maps. Inbound data bypasses quality controls.
recommendation: Always use transform maps with import sets to validate, cleanse, and map incoming data to target tables. Direct table imports bypass data quality controls.

name: Heavy Scheduled Job Usage (Potential Polling)
description: Instance has a high number of scheduled script executions, suggesting polling-based integration patterns instead of event-driven.
condition_desc: More than 50 scheduled scripts configured, suggesting polling patterns
message_template: {sysauto_script_count} scheduled scripts detected. Review for polling patterns that could be replaced with event-driven triggers.
recommendation: Replace polling-based integrations with event-driven patterns where possible. Use Business Rules, Flow Designer triggers, or inbound webhooks to react to changes in real-time without unnecessary polling overhead.

name: No MID Server for On-Premise Integration
description: Instance integrates with on-premise systems without a MID Server.
condition_desc: Outbound integrations target private/internal IP ranges but no active MID Server is configured
message_template: No active MID Servers detected. On-premise integrations have no secure channel.
recommendation: Deploy a MID Server for all on-premise integrations. Direct connections from cloud to on-premise bypass security controls and are unreliable. MID Server provides secure, managed connectivity.

name: Legacy Workflow Instead of Flow Designer
description: Instance uses legacy Workflow Engine where Flow Designer should be used.
condition_desc: Active legacy workflows (wf_workflow) found that could be migrated to Flow Designer
message_template: Legacy Workflow Engine has {wf_workflow_count} workflows vs {sys_hub_flow_count} Flow Designer flows. Migration recommended.
recommendation: Migrate legacy workflows to Flow Designer. Flow Designer provides better error handling, Integration Hub support, and is ServiceNow's strategic direction for process automation. Legacy Workflow is in maintenance mode.

name: Email-Based Integration
description: Instance uses inbound email actions for integration, which is fragile and difficult to monitor compared to REST API or Import Sets.
condition_desc: Inbound email actions exist for processing external data
message_template: {sysevent_email_action_count} inbound email actions detected. Consider replacing with REST API or Import Set integrations.
recommendation: Email-based integration is a fallback pattern. Replace with REST API or Import Set integrations for reliable, structured data exchange. Email is fragile, difficult to monitor, and lacks transactional guarantees.

name: SOAP-Only Integration Stack — No REST Adoption
description: Instance has SOAP integrations but zero REST message configurations. Per Jochen Geist's protocol hierarchy (REST > GraphQL > SOAP > ODBC), this represents a severely outdated integration stack.
condition_desc: SOAP messages present but no REST messages at all — entire integration stack is on a deprecated protocol
message_template: {sys_soap_message_count} SOAP integrations but zero REST messages. Entire integration stack bypasses the preferred REST protocol.
recommendation: Prioritize REST API adoption. Per the Integration Pattern Decision Tree, REST is the default protocol for 80% of integrations. SOAP should only remain for systems that offer no REST alternative. Establish a migration roadmap starting with the highest-traffic SOAP endpoints.

name: Integration Sprawl Without Centralized Orchestration
description: Instance has multiple active integration types (REST, SOAP, JDBC, import sets) but no Integration Hub for centralized governance. Per Jochen Geist's governance framework, this creates an unmanaged integration labyrinth.
condition_desc: 3+ distinct integration pattern types active without Integration Hub — integration sprawl with no central governance
message_template: Integration sprawl: REST + import sets + SOAP/JDBC all active without Integration Hub. No centralized orchestration or governance.
recommendation: Deploy Integration Hub to centralize integration orchestration, monitoring, and error handling. Per Integration Governance principles: standardize integration intake, visualize dependencies, and align with enterprise architecture. Without centralized governance, each integration becomes an isolated technical debt item.

name: JDBC Consolidation Opportunity — Zero Copy Candidate
description: Instance has 5+ JDBC data sources, suggesting heavy reliance on direct database connectivity. Per Jochen Geist's decision tree, Zero Copy Connectors should be considered for data residency and high-volume access without duplicating data into ServiceNow.
condition_desc: 5+ JDBC data sources — significant external database dependency that may benefit from Zero Copy Connectors
message_template: {sys_data_source_count} JDBC data sources. Heavy external DB dependency — evaluate Zero Copy Connectors to reduce replication overhead.
recommendation: Evaluate Zero Copy Connectors (ZCC) for supported databases (SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, SAP HANA, Redshift, BigQuery, Databricks). ZCC provides read access to external data without replication, reducing data staleness and platform load. Per the decision tree: Zero Copy is preferred when data residency, compliance, or volume constraints apply.

name: Polling Pattern Detected — Event-Driven Architecture Opportunity
description: Instance has very high scheduled script count combined with high import set volume, suggesting batch/polling integration patterns. Per Jochen Geist's decision tree, event-driven architecture (Stream Connect, webhooks, Flow Designer triggers) should replace polling for real-time requirements.
condition_desc: 100+ scheduled scripts AND 100+ import sets — strong signal of batch/polling integration architecture
message_template: {sysauto_script_count} scheduled scripts + {sys_import_set_count} import sets. Polling/batch architecture — evaluate Stream Connect or event-driven patterns.
recommendation: Replace polling-based integrations with event-driven patterns. Use Stream Connect (Apache Kafka) for high-volume real-time event streaming, or Flow Designer triggers and webhooks for lower-volume event processing. Per the decision tree: event-driven architecture provides decoupled, scalable communication that eliminates polling overhead and latency.

name: Potential Direct Table API Writes — Import Set API Preferred
description: Instance has many REST message configurations but very few import sets or transform maps, suggesting inbound data may use direct Table API writes. Per Jochen Geist: Write operations preference is Import Set API > Scripted REST API > Direct Table API writes (Avoid).
condition_desc: 10+ REST messages but <= 3 import sets and <= 3 transform maps — inbound data likely bypasses Import Set API data quality controls
message_template: {sys_rest_message_count} REST messages but only {sys_import_set_count} import sets / {sys_transform_map_count} transform maps. Inbound writes may bypass Import Set data quality controls.
recommendation: Use Import Set API with transform maps for all inbound data writes. Import Sets provide staging, validation, coalescing, and rollback capabilities. Direct Table API writes bypass these controls and risk data corruption. Per the Integration Design methodology: "Import Set API > Direct Table API writes."

name: Scripted REST API Proliferation — Governance Risk
description: Instance has many custom Scripted REST API operations, expanding the API surface area without standardized governance. Per Jochen Geist's governance framework: establish platform-wide standards and document integrations.
condition_desc: 10+ scripted REST API operations — significant custom API surface area requiring governance
message_template: {sys_ws_operation_count} custom Scripted REST API operations. Significant API surface area — establish governance and consolidation roadmap.
recommendation: Audit custom Scripted REST APIs for consolidation and governance. Each custom API operation adds to the maintenance burden, security surface, and upgrade risk. Consider whether Integration Hub or standard Table/Import Set APIs could replace custom endpoints. Document all custom APIs and establish an integration intake process.

name: Integration Hub Present But Custom Scripts Dominate
description: Integration Hub is installed but custom REST messages significantly outnumber IHub flows, indicating the OOTB-first investment is underutilized. Per Jochen Geist: "Reduced development time, lower maintenance burden, automatic updates, proven reliability" are the benefits of leveraging IHub spokes.
condition_desc: Integration Hub active but custom REST messages > 2x IHub flows — IHub investment underutilized
message_template: Integration Hub active but {sys_rest_message_count} custom REST messages vs {sys_hub_flow_count} IHub flows. Custom scripts dominate — check available spokes.
recommendation: Review custom REST integrations for IHub spoke replacements. ServiceNow maintains hundreds of pre-built spokes with automatic updates, built-in error handling, and connection management. Migrating custom integrations to spokes reduces maintenance burden and unlocks Integration Hub governance capabilities (monitoring, retry policies, execution history).

name: CMDB Not Populated by Automated Discovery
description: CMDB has records but no Discovery or Service Mapping activity detected.
condition_desc: cmdb_ci has records but discovery_status table is empty or Discovery plugin is inactive
message_template: CMDB has {total_cis} CIs but no automated Discovery. Data staleness risk.
recommendation: CMDB data maintained manually becomes stale quickly. Enable Discovery and Service Mapping for automated population and ongoing maintenance.

name: Customer Portal Connected to ITSM Directly
description: Public-facing Customer Portal creates records in internal ITSM tables.
condition_desc: Customer Portal has catalog items or forms that create incident or sc_request records directly instead of CSM cases
message_template: Customer Service plugin installed but no CSM cases — customers may be routed to ITSM directly.
recommendation: Public/customer-facing requests should flow through CSM (Customer Service Management), not directly into ITSM. ITSM is for internal IT operations. CSM provides customer context, SLA management, and proper segregation.

name: Domain Separation Not Enabled
description: Domain separation is not enabled. Multi-tenant or multi-BU instances require domain separation to enforce data isolation.
condition_desc: glide.sys.domain.enabled property is not set to true
message_template: Domain separation is not enabled. Data isolation between business units is not enforced.
recommendation: Enable domain separation for multi-tenant environments to enforce data isolation between business units. Without it, data leakage across tenants is a significant risk.

name: No Agent Workspace — ITSM Agents on Classic UI
description: Instance has active ITSM with significant ticket volume but agents use legacy list/form UI instead of Agent Workspace.
condition_desc: ITSM active with 500+ incidents but Agent Workspace not installed — agents processing significant volume without modern workspace
message_template: ITSM active with {incident_count} incidents but no Agent Workspace. Agents on classic UI.
recommendation: Migrate agents to Agent Workspace for improved productivity. With {incident_count} incidents processed through classic UI, agents miss contextual side panels, AI-assisted resolution, and multi-tab workflows. Workspace is ServiceNow's strategic direction for agent experience.

name: Legacy Workflow Engine Still Active
description: Legacy Workflow Engine has active workflows that should be on Flow Designer.
condition_desc: wf_workflow table has active workflows created in the last 12 months
message_template: {wf_workflow_count} legacy workflows still active. Flow Designer is the strategic direction.
recommendation: New automation should use Flow Designer, not Legacy Workflow. Plan migration of active legacy workflows to Flow Designer for better maintainability and access to Integration Hub capabilities.

name: Incident Without Problem Management
description: Incident Management is active but Problem Management is not used. The D2C value stream requires a root cause analysis loop.
condition_desc: Incident table has records but Problem plugin is inactive or problem table has zero records
message_template: Incident Management is active but Problem Management is not in use. No root cause analysis loop.
recommendation: Enable Problem Management to close the reactive-to-proactive loop. Without root cause analysis, the same incidents recur. Problem Management is included in the ITSM license at no additional cost.

name: ITSM Without Knowledge Management
description: ITSM is active but Knowledge Base has fewer than 10 articles. Knowledge deflection is the highest-ROI self-service investment.
condition_desc: ITSM plugins active and incident table has records, but Knowledge plugin is inactive or kb_knowledge has < 10 articles
message_template: ITSM is active but Knowledge Base has only {kb_knowledge_count} articles. Self-service deflection opportunity missed.
recommendation: Build a Knowledge Base from resolved incidents. ServiceNow's Knowledge Management allows agents to create KB articles during incident resolution. Even 50 well-written articles can deflect 15-20% of ticket volume.

name: Service Catalog Without Virtual Agent
description: Service Catalog is active but Virtual Agent is not installed. VA provides conversational self-service for common requests.
condition_desc: Service Catalog plugin is active and sc_cat_item has records, but Virtual Agent (com.glide.cs.chatbot) is not installed
message_template: Service Catalog is active but Virtual Agent is not installed. Conversational self-service not available.
recommendation: Deploy Virtual Agent to guide users to the right catalog items conversationally. VA integrates with Service Catalog and Knowledge Base to deflect common requests before they become tickets.

name: HRSD Without Employee Center
description: HR Service Delivery is installed but Employee Center is not. Employees lack a unified portal for HR services.
condition_desc: HRSD plugin (com.sn_hr_core) is installed but Employee Center (com.sn_employee_center) is not installed
message_template: HRSD is installed but Employee Center is not. Employees lack a unified, personalized portal for HR services.
recommendation: Deploy Employee Center to give employees a single front door for HR services, knowledge, and cases. Employee Center is ServiceNow's strategic replacement for Service Portal in HR workflows and provides personalized, department-specific content delivery.

name: CSM Without Dedicated Customer Portal
description: Customer Service Management is installed but no portal is configured for external customer self-service.
condition_desc: CSM plugin (com.sn_customerservice) is installed but Service Portal (com.glide.service-portal) is not installed
message_template: CSM is installed but no Customer Portal for customer self-service. Cases can only be created by agents.
recommendation: Deploy a Customer Portal (via Service Portal) to provide external customers with self-service case management, knowledge search, and request tracking. Without a portal, CSM cases can only be created by agents, losing the deflection benefits.

name: Discovery Without Service Mapping
description: Discovery is active but Service Mapping is not. Infrastructure CIs are discovered but service context is missing.
condition_desc: Discovery plugin (com.snc.discovery) is installed but Service Mapping (com.snc.service_mapping) is not installed
message_template: Discovery is active but Service Mapping is not. Infrastructure CIs are discovered but service-level topology is missing.
recommendation: Add Service Mapping to Discovery to build application-level service maps. Discovery finds individual CIs but Service Mapping connects them into business services, enabling impact analysis during incidents and changes.

name: CMDB Without Asset Management
description: CMDB is populated but Asset Management is not active. No lifecycle tracking for hardware and software assets.
condition_desc: cmdb_ci has records but Asset Management plugin (com.snc.asset_management) is not installed
message_template: CMDB has {total_cis} CIs but Asset Management is not active. No lifecycle tracking for hardware and software assets.
recommendation: Enable IT Asset Management (ITAM) to track asset lifecycle — procurement, deployment, maintenance, and retirement. CMDB tells you what you have; ITAM tells you what it costs and when to replace it. HAM and SAM provide hardware and software license compliance.

name: Security Incident Response Without Vulnerability Response
description: SIR is installed but Vulnerability Response is not. Reactive-only security posture without proactive vulnerability management.
condition_desc: Security Incident Response plugin (com.snc.sec_inc_response) is installed but Vulnerability Response (com.snc.vul_response) is not
message_template: Security Incident Response is active but Vulnerability Response is not. Reactive-only security posture.
recommendation: Add Vulnerability Response to shift from reactive (incident-only) to proactive security operations. VR integrates with scanners (Qualys, Tenable, Rapid7) to prioritize and track vulnerability remediation before exploitation.

name: SPM Without GRC
description: Strategic Portfolio Management is active but GRC is not. Portfolio decisions lack governance and risk oversight.
condition_desc: SPM/ITBM plugin (com.snc.project_management) is installed but GRC plugins (com.sn_compliance, com.sn_risk) are not installed
message_template: SPM is active but GRC is not. Portfolio decisions lack governance oversight.
recommendation: Add Integrated Risk Management (GRC) alongside SPM to incorporate risk assessments and compliance requirements into portfolio decisions. This ensures investment priorities account for regulatory obligations.

name: No SecOps on Instance With Sensitive Data
description: Instance processes sensitive data (ITSM/CSM/HRSD) but has no Security Operations capability for threat detection and response.
condition_desc: Instance has ITSM, CSM, or HRSD active (processing PII/sensitive data) but neither Security Incident Response nor Vulnerability Response is installed
message_template: Instance processes sensitive data (ITSM/CSM/HRSD) but has no Security Operations capability.
recommendation: Consider Security Operations (SecOps) to protect the ServiceNow instance and its data. SIR provides security incident workflows, and VR enables proactive vulnerability management. Both integrate with SIEM/SOAR tools.

name: Potential Shelfware — HRSD Installed But No HR Cases
description: HR Service Delivery plugin is installed but hr_core_case table has zero records. License may be underutilized.
condition_desc: HRSD plugin (com.sn_hr_core) is installed but sn_hr_core_case table has zero records
message_template: HRSD is active but sn_hr_core_case has zero records. License may be underutilized.
recommendation: HRSD is licensed but not producing HR cases. Either onboard HR teams to use the platform, or evaluate whether the license is needed. Unused licenses represent significant annual cost with zero ROI.

name: Potential Shelfware — CSM Installed But No Cases
description: Customer Service Management plugin is installed but case table has zero records. License may be underutilized.
condition_desc: CSM plugin (com.sn_customerservice) is installed but sn_customerservice_case table has zero records
message_template: CSM is active but sn_customerservice_case has zero records. License may be underutilized.
recommendation: CSM is licensed but not producing customer cases. Either complete the CSM implementation (portal, assignment rules, SLAs) or evaluate whether the license is still needed.

name: Potential Shelfware — SecOps Installed But No Incidents
description: Security Incident Response plugin is installed but SIR table has zero records. Security automation not operational.
condition_desc: SIR plugin (com.snc.sec_inc_response) is installed but sn_si_incident table has zero records
message_template: Security Incident Response is active but sn_si_incident has zero records. SIEM integration not connected.
recommendation: Security Incident Response is licensed but not ingesting security events. Connect SIR to your SIEM (Splunk, Sentinel, etc.) to automate security incident creation and response workflows.

name: Flow Designer Underutilized
description: Flow Designer is installed but has fewer flows than legacy workflows. Modern automation capabilities are underutilized.
condition_desc: Flow Designer plugin is active but sys_hub_flow count is less than half of wf_workflow count
message_template: Flow Designer has {sys_hub_flow_count} flows vs {wf_workflow_count} legacy workflows. Modern automation underutilized.
recommendation: Prioritize new automations in Flow Designer over Legacy Workflow. Flow Designer supports Integration Hub actions, error policies, and parallel execution. Plan a migration roadmap for legacy workflows starting with the most frequently triggered ones.
